commonIssues:
Difficulty in providing old family records or pedigree charts (Vanshavruksha) to prove caste lineage, especially in desert and tribal areas.

How to fix:

  • Obtain old school records or admission registers of parents/grandparents.
  • Request Jamabandi or Khasra-Girdawari records from the Patwari — these may mention caste.
  • Submit a sworn affidavit from two community elders along with their own caste certificates.
  • Apply via Rajasthan SSO Portal.

Login failures, OTP issues, document upload errors, and server timeouts on the Rajasthan SSO or e-Mitra portals.

How to fix:

  • Use Google Chrome (latest version) and clear cache before accessing the portal.
  • Upload documents in JPEG/PDF under 200KB — compress using SmallPDF.
  • Try during off-peak hours (early morning).
  • Visit the nearest e-Mitra Kiosk for assisted filing if online access fails.

Applications pending at Patwari or Tehsildar level for months due to backlog or inaction.

How to fix:

  • Track status on Rajasthan SSO Portal using your application number.
  • File a grievance at Rajasthan Sampark Grievance Portal if delayed beyond 15 days.
  • Escalate to the District Collector office or attend the Jansunavai.
  • Invoke the Rajasthan Guaranteed Delivery of Public Services Act.

Tribal communities in areas like Udaipur, Banswara, and Dungarpur face additional scrutiny and lengthy verification for ST certificates.

How to fix:

  • Submit Tribal Development Department records or forest dwelling certificates as supplementary proof.
  • Provide community certificates of relatives already verified by the government.
  • Contact the Tribal Area Development Commissioner for assistance.
  • Seek help from NALSA for free legal aid.

Errors in the issued certificate such as misspelled names, wrong caste name, or incorrect district details.

How to fix:

  • Apply for certificate correction at the Tehsil office with supporting documents.
  • Submit Aadhaar, school records, and family's existing certificates as evidence.
  • For online certificates, request re-issuance through SSO Portal.
  • If correction is refused, escalate to the District Collector's office.

Applications rejected without providing a clear written explanation.

How to fix:

  • Request a written reason for rejection from the Tehsildar.
  • File an appeal with the Sub-Divisional Officer (SDO) within 30 days.
  • Lodge a grievance at Sampark Portal.
  • Contact DLSA for free legal aid — NALSA Portal.

OBC applicants face hurdles proving non-creamy layer status, especially self-employed families.

How to fix:

  • Apply for NCL certificate separately via SSO Portal.
  • Provide 3-year Income Tax Returns or CA-certified income certificate.
  • If parents are salaried, submit salary slips and Form-16.
  • Current NCL limit is ₹8 lakhs per annum — check latest DoPT notification.

e-Mitra kiosk operators often charge ₹200-₹500 extra beyond the prescribed government fee.

How to fix:

  • Check the official fee structure on e-Mitra Portal before visiting a kiosk.
  • Apply directly online via Rajasthan SSO Portal to avoid kiosk charges.
  • Report overcharging to the District Collector or Sampark Portal.
  • Always collect a printed receipt showing the exact fee charged.
